task_summary: Refactor admin_chat to route messages by author identity (admin→agent, pending-recipient→state machine, else→passthrough), add a new `awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation` intent state with keyword-based confirm/negative/ambiguous parsing, add query_*/initiate_batch_payment/upsert_wallet_for_user agent tools, introduce an admin-DM-only AdminApprovalView approval gate for real admin_chat payments (replacing PaymentConfirmView for that producer only), auto-skip the test phase for verified wallets, and add a 24h timeout escalation. Grants flow and all hardening/cleanup/polish invariants stay intact.

key_evidence.point: Today admin_chat routing is content-based: on_message first calls _check_pending_payment_reply which intercepts any recipient reply in any channel for any active intent, then falls through to mention/DM detection and agent invocation. Identity routing replaces both branches with a single author-id switch.

Current intent status CHECK constraint is ('awaiting_wallet','awaiting_test','awaiting_confirmation','confirmed','completed','failed','cancelled'). No 'awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' exists; a SQL migration must add it to the CHECK constraint.

Today's flow creates a real test payment and AUTO-confirms it via PaymentActorKind.AUTO in _start_admin_payment_flow, then on terminal test success creates the real payment and calls PaymentUICog.send_confirmation_request for recipient confirmation. The new flow must instead park the intent in awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation after the test confirms on-chain, then wait for the recipient's text ack before creating the real payment.

PaymentConfirmView is used for BOTH grants and admin_chat real payments today — both grants_cog.py:719 and admin_chat_cog.py:393 call payment_ui_cog.send_confirmation_request. The refactor must NOT drop the grants call site, only the admin_chat call site.

Reply classification today uses a Claude-based _classify_payment_reply call per message; the new pending-recipient state machine must be deterministic with zero LLM calls. The task mandates hardcoded regex/keyword matching for both wallet detection and confirmation keywords.
is_valid_solana_address already exists and does base58 decode + 32-byte length check — reuse it rather than re-implementing. It's imported in admin_chat_cog and used at :586,:592.

execute_initiate_payment already has wallet-verification auto-skip logic: it filters wallet_record by verified_at and only calls _start_admin_payment_flow when a verified wallet is present. However, _start_admin_payment_flow currently issues the TEST payment unconditionally — it never 'goes straight to real'. The auto-skip must be implemented by branching at intent creation time, not inside _start_admin_payment_flow.

DB layer is Supabase REST — there is no generic multi-row transaction helper on DatabaseHandler. create_admin_payment_intent inserts one row per call. Atomic batch creation (initiate_batch_payment) will require either (a) a new Postgres RPC function wrapping N inserts in a txn, or (b) a new db_handler.create_admin_payment_intents_batch method that uses a single Supabase insert([...]) call plus an explicit rollback path on partial failure. Supabase .insert([list]) is atomic per-request on PostgREST but only within one table — sufficient here since all rows are admin_payment_intents.

PaymentUICog persistent-view registration pattern: cog_load → _register_pending_confirmation_views → bot.add_view(PaymentConfirmView(self, payment_id)) for every pending payment. The new AdminApprovalView must follow the same shape, keyed by payment_id (not intent_id) with custom_id='payment_admin_approve:{payment_id}' so restart recovery works.

ADMIN_USER_ID is an env var read in admin_chat_cog.py:50 (cached as self.admin_user_id) AND separately in payment_ui_cog.py:165. The AdminApprovalView button callback must independently re-check the interacting user matches ADMIN_USER_ID (defense-in-depth — custom_id can't be spoofed but discord.py permissions model is view-level).

safe_delete_messages lives in src/common/discord_utils.py:350-451 with signature (channel, message_ids, *, logger) returning DeleteCounts. Already used by payment_worker_cog.py:172. Reuse for 'delete stale flow messages in the thread' after approval.

Startup reconciliation loop in admin_chat_cog only walks {awaiting_wallet, awaiting_confirmation, awaiting_test, confirmed} branches. Must be extended to recognize 'awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' and the new 'awaiting_admin_approval' state, and to fire the 24h timeout sweep.

There is NO existing 24-hour timeout loop — only a 30s payment worker loop (payment_worker_cog.py:92) and startup reconciliation. A new background task (either in AdminChatCog or a dedicated IntentTimeoutCog) must be added to scan awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation rows older than 24h and escalate.

Wallet upsert today goes through db_handler.upsert_wallet with a WalletUpdateBlockedError guard when an active payment intent exists for the user. The new upsert_wallet_for_user tool must respect this guard (the admin must see the error), and must NOT set verified_at — that only happens after a successful test round-trip via existing mark_wallet_verified at payment_service.py:910,967.

Current agent initiate_payment tool definition at tools.py:580-595 takes (recipient_user_id, amount_sol, reason?). The new initiate_batch_payment tool takes a payments[] array; per the task, batches MUST be implemented so that per-payment mechanics (auto-skip, test cycle, admin DM) fan out individually — a batch of 16 produces 16 DMs, not one. This implies batch is really 'create N intents atomically then let each flow through the existing per-intent pipeline'.

The agent has no read-only payment/wallet state tools today. It has list_payments/list_wallets/get_payment_status (write-admin tools under 'Doing things' in the system prompt) but these are generic ledger inspectors. The task asks for three NEW narrowly-scoped tools (query_payment_state, query_wallet_state, list_recent_payments) — these are net-new, not renames of existing tools. Keep existing list_payments/list_wallets/get_payment_status in place; add the new three alongside.

Under identity-based routing, approved-member messages (the _can_user_message_bot path at admin_chat_cog.py:694-714) stop reaching the agent unless the author is ADMIN_USER_ID — an intentional scoping reduction. MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:151) becomes effectively dead code; do not delete it, but note in the plan that the member branch is disabled at the cog level.

Reconciliation at startup walks discord_messages and re-invokes _check_pending_payment_reply per recipient message. After the refactor, _check_pending_payment_reply is replaced by the identity router's pending-recipient branch, but the startup reconciler must still be able to invoke it synthetically to catch up on missed messages during downtime.

handle_payment_result currently creates the final payment inline in the test-success branch. After the refactor, test-success no longer creates the final payment — it transitions the intent to awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation and waits for the recipient's text. The real payment is created only when the state machine processes a positive confirmation, and then transitions to awaiting_admin_approval (new state) which triggers the admin DM.

Existing test file test_admin_payments.py (1350 lines) has dense coverage of the current flow: wallet classification via Claude, WalletUpdateBlockedError, auto-confirm of test payments, final payment flow. Many tests will need updating because the state machine transitions and reply handling change fundamentally. Flag as 'expect broad test churn in test_admin_payments.py'.

Hardening invariants to preserve: per-user-per-day caps, canonical wallet swap defense, idempotency collision detection, WalletUpdateBlockedError, RLS, prompt-injection hardening (wallet lookups only from wallet_registry — NEVER from LLM-provided strings), null-recipient fail-closed. The new batch tool and upsert tool must also never accept an LLM-sourced wallet address without going through wallet_registry/upsert_wallet — the existing 'ignore injected wallet_address' pattern at tools.py:2501-2508 is the template.

relevant_code.why: The cog housing on_message, the content-based interceptor that must become identity-based, the existing state handlers (_handle_wallet_received, _start_admin_payment_flow, _handle_confirmation_received, handle_payment_result), startup reconciliation, and admin_user_id config. This file is the epicenter of the refactor.

Holds all Claude tool schemas and executors. New tools (query_payment_state, query_wallet_state, list_recent_payments, initiate_batch_payment, upsert_wallet_for_user) are declared here; execute_initiate_payment will need the auto-skip-for-verified-wallet branch, and the LLM-wallet-injection guard pattern is reused by new tools.
functions: execute tool dispatch (dispatch_tool / execute_tool around line 3198)

relevant_code.why: SYSTEM_PROMPT must gain a new 'State questions → query_* first' section; tool list in prompt must list the new tools; MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT becomes dead-branch under identity routing.

PaymentConfirmView and the persistent-view registration pattern live here. The new AdminApprovalView can be added to this file (next to PaymentConfirmView) and re-registered on cog_load via a new _register_pending_admin_approval_views pass that queries admin_payment_intents with status=awaiting_admin_approval. send_confirmation_request must be gated so it is NOT called for producer='admin_chat' + is_test=False (either at the call site in admin_chat_cog.py or inside send_confirmation_request itself — prefer call site since grants keeps using it unchanged).

request_payment is invoked to create both test and real payment rows; mark_wallet_verified at :910,:967 is the ONLY place verified_at is set today — this must remain the single write path (the state machine triggers it by calling confirm_payment on the test row OR a new explicit db_handler.mark_wallet_verified call when the recipient text-acks).

Background worker loop structure. Either (a) add the 24h intent-timeout sweep as a new task here, or (b) add it to AdminChatCog. Payment_worker_cog is the existing 'cron-ish' home and has recovery/reconciliation precedent.

All admin_payment_intent CRUD + wallet_registry CRUD. Must add: mark_wallet_verified explicit call (already exists at :1649 — verify the signature), a new create_admin_payment_intents_batch (Supabase insert([list])), and query helpers for the new agent tools. upsert_wallet is already WalletUpdateBlockedError-aware.

Status CHECK constraint must be extended to include 'awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' and 'awaiting_admin_approval'. The unique index on (guild_id, channel_id, recipient_user_id) WHERE status NOT IN (completed, failed, cancelled) already excludes terminal states — new non-terminal states are automatically covered, but verify.
is_valid_solana_address — reuse for the state machine's wallet regex parsing (do NOT re-implement). Already used at admin_chat_cog.py:586 and :592.
VERIFY grants does not regress. It uses payment_ui_cog.send_confirmation_request at :719 for its own producer='grants' flow — that call site must be preserved untouched.
safe_delete_messages primitive; reuse in AdminApprovalView click handler to delete stale flow messages in the thread.

Primary existing test file (1350 lines). Expect substantial updates: every test that mocked _classify_payment_reply must be rewritten around the deterministic keyword parser; tests that assert the awaiting_confirmation state transition must switch to awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation; PaymentConfirmView expectations for admin_chat must move to AdminApprovalView.
functions: existing tests for wallet classification, WalletUpdateBlockedError, test→final payment transition, confirmation flow

relevant_code.why: Hypothesis-based payment_requests state machine tests. Must remain green — the refactor does not change payment_requests states, only the admin_payment_intents state machine and the UI layer.
Tests the PaymentWorkerCog/PaymentUICog split. Must remain green; the new AdminApprovalView additions to PaymentUICog should not break its cog_load or view-registration tests.

test_expectations.what_it_checks: When author.id == ADMIN_USER_ID, on_message routes to AdminChatAgent.chat regardless of message content (including Solana-address-shaped text).
When author has an active non-terminal admin_payment_intent, on_message runs the deterministic state machine with zero LLM calls (assert _classify_payment_reply / anthropic client is NOT invoked).
When author is neither admin nor pending recipient, admin_chat cog does nothing and other cogs receive the message.
awaiting_wallet + valid base58 32-byte address → upsert wallet (verified_at=NULL), transition to awaiting_test, trigger test payment.
awaiting_wallet + non-address text → no action, no reply, intent stays in awaiting_wallet (no spam).

awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation + each of ['confirmed','received','got it','yes','yep','confirm','👍'] → mark wallet_registry.verified_at=now, transition to awaiting_admin_approval, create real payment, fire admin DM.
awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation + ['no','didnt','not received','missing','nothing'] → tag admin in thread with tx signature + recipient wallet + intent_id, intent transitions to manual_review.
First ambiguous reply → silent ignore, state unchanged; second ambiguous reply → admin tag. Counter persisted on intent row.
initiate_payment with recipient having wallet_registry.verified_at IS NOT NULL skips the test payment entirely — no is_test=True row is created, a real payment row goes to awaiting_admin_approval.
initiate_batch_payment([p1,p2,p3]) with p2 invalid → zero intents persisted (rollback). Valid batch → all N intents created, exactly N admin DMs fire (one per payment, not per batch).
upsert_wallet_for_user rejects non-admin callers; on success creates wallet_registry row with verified_at=NULL and DMs the admin a confirmation.
When an active intent exists for the user, upsert_wallet_for_user surfaces WalletUpdateBlockedError to the admin.
AdminApprovalView button rejects any non-ADMIN_USER_ID interaction with an ephemeral denial, does not advance payment.
PaymentUICog.cog_load re-registers AdminApprovalView for every admin_payment_intent in awaiting_admin_approval (custom_id routing survives restart).
Admin click: edits DM to ✅ approved, sends follow-up DM, posts thread reply, deletes stale flow messages via safe_delete_messages, calls payment_service.confirm_payment to queue the real payment.
discord.Forbidden on DM send → intent stays in awaiting_admin_approval, ERROR log emitted, no fallback to public channel.
PaymentUICog.send_confirmation_request is NOT called for producer='admin_chat' is_test=False payments (either gated or not-reached); still called for grants.
Background sweep finds admin_payment_intents with status=awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation + updated_at < now-24h → transitions to manual_review and DMs admin.
query_payment_state returns canonical DB state for a payment id; does not mutate.
query_wallet_state returns wallet_registry rows for a user with verified_at/created_at; does not mutate.
Query tools only source wallets from wallet_registry — never from LLM-provided strings (per existing hardening invariant).
Under identity routing, an approved (can_message_bot=true) non-admin member message does NOT invoke the agent. Intentional scoping reduction.
Hypothesis-driven payment_requests state transitions — payment_requests states are unchanged by this refactor; must remain green.
PaymentWorkerCog/PaymentUICog cog_load and persistent view re-registration — must remain green; AdminApprovalView additions should not break PaymentConfirmView re-registration.
Solana client + is_valid_solana_address — must remain green; state machine reuses is_valid_solana_address.
Idempotency collision detection — must remain green.
Reconciliation with on-chain truth — must remain green.
Payment authorization/wallet swap defense — must remain green.
safe_delete_messages primitive — must remain green; AdminApprovalView reuses it.
Invariant checker script — must remain green; new states must not trip invariants.
tx_signature_history column invariants — must remain green.

constraints: MUST NOT touch solana_client.py / solana_provider.py — the rebroadcast loop, priority fees, status.err handling all stay.

MUST NOT touch grants_cog. Grants keeps its own flow (test auto-confirmed by bot, recipient clicks PaymentConfirmView button). grants_cog.py:719 call to send_confirmation_request MUST remain functional.

MUST preserve hardening invariants: per-user-per-day caps, canonical wallet swap defense, idempotency collision detection, WalletUpdateBlockedError, RLS comments, prompt-injection hardening (wallet lookups only from wallet_registry), null-recipient fail-closed in PaymentService.confirm_payment.
MUST preserve cleanup invariants: PaymentActor/PRODUCER_FLOWS registry, tx_signature_history column, PaymentWorkerCog/PaymentUICog split, safe_delete_messages primitive.

MUST preserve polish invariants: reconcile_with_chain, dynamic priority fees, /payment-resolve slash command, provider name migration, docs/payments.md, docs/runbook-payments.md, scripts/check_payment_invariants.py, structured tx_confirm_decision logging.

Agent MUST NEVER accept LLM-sourced wallet addresses. initiate_batch_payment and upsert_wallet_for_user must reuse the existing 'pop and warn' guard pattern from execute_initiate_payment at tools.py:2501-2508 (for the batch), and upsert_wallet_for_user must take the wallet_address explicitly from a validated admin parameter (not from unrelated LLM context).

verified_at MUST only be set after a real round-trip: on-chain test confirmation + recipient text acknowledgment. Neither alone is sufficient. upsert_wallet_for_user creates the row with verified_at=NULL.
Admin DM approval gate fires ONLY for real (is_test=False) payments under producer='admin_chat'. NEVER for is_test=True. NEVER for producer='grants'.
One admin DM per payment (not per batch). A batch of 16 → 16 DMs. Each DM carries its own AdminApprovalView with a unique custom_id.
AdminApprovalView must be persistent (timeout=None) and re-registered on bot startup via cog_load for restart recovery. Pattern: match PaymentConfirmView registration in payment_ui_cog.py.

PaymentConfirmView must NO LONGER fire for producer='admin_chat' real payments. Grants keeps using it. The gating should be at the call site in admin_chat_cog.py (remove/replace the send_confirmation_request call) — do NOT add a producer check inside send_confirmation_request because that would couple PaymentUICog to producer semantics.

Existing admin_payment_intents schema: a SQL migration must add 'awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' and 'awaiting_admin_approval' to the status CHECK constraint; migration must be idempotent (DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS / ADD CONSTRAINT) and ship with the code change.

Identity router for approved-member messages: under the new routing, approved members' messages are no longer forwarded to the agent unless they are the admin. Document explicitly — this is an intentional scoping reduction per the task spec. MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT is NOT deleted (dead code left in place).

Keyword lists for confirmation/negative/ambiguous MUST be hardcoded in code (no LLM call). Case-insensitive match, whole-word boundary preferred, include emoji variants ('👍'). Ambiguous = neither positive nor negative.

24h timeout: new background task (add to AdminChatCog or PaymentWorkerCog) that scans admin_payment_intents with status=awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation AND updated_at < now-24h, transitions to manual_review, and DMs admin. Interval ≥ 5 minutes to avoid DB hammering.

Batch tool atomicity: prefer Supabase .insert([list]) (PostgREST atomic per-request) for create_admin_payment_intents_batch. If any row fails validation BEFORE insert, reject the whole batch with zero DB writes. If the DB rejects the multi-row insert, the whole request fails atomically.

Test churn expected: test_admin_payments.py (1350 lines) will see broad rewrites. New tests MUST NOT mock _classify_payment_reply (it's no longer called from the state machine path). They SHOULD continue to assert WalletUpdateBlockedError paths.
DO NOT add a cog-layer rejection of agent replies that make state claims without query-tool calls. Per the task: system-prompt guidance only, no enforcement.
DO NOT start nested megaplan plans or invoke the megaplan CLI from within implementation — treat megaplan mentions as context only.
suggested_approach:
Land this in phased, reviewable chunks that keep tests green at every step.

PHASE 1 — Schema + state enum (smallest diff, lowest risk):
(a) SQL migration: extend admin_payment_intents status CHECK to add 'awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' and 'awaiting_admin_approval'. Idempotent DROP/ADD CONSTRAINT pattern. Update sql/admin_payment_intents.sql + any migration runner.
(b) db_handler helpers: add create_admin_payment_intents_batch (Supabase .insert([list])), a list_intents_by_status helper, a list_stale_test_receipt_intents(cutoff) for the 24h sweep, and an ambiguous-reply counter update path.

PHASE 2 — Identity router (on_message rewrite):
Replace the current on_message + _check_pending_payment_reply branching with a single switch:
 1. If author.id == ADMIN_USER_ID → existing agent path (unchanged except new tools added in phase 4).
 2. Else if db_handler.get_active_intent_for_recipient returns a row → dispatch to a new _handle_pending_recipient_message(intent, message) that routes by intent.status (awaiting_wallet, awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation). No LLM calls.
 3. Else → return without handling (fallthrough).
Delete the Claude-based _classify_payment_reply code path from the message-handler flow (keep the function around for the phase-2 diff small; delete in a later cleanup commit if truly unused).
Add deterministic helpers:
 - `_parse_wallet_from_text(content) -> str | None` reusing is_valid_solana_address.
 - `_classify_confirmation(content) -> Literal['positive','negative','ambiguous']` with hardcoded keyword lists from the task spec (whole-word, case-insensitive, emoji-aware).

PHASE 3 — Flow rewiring (test-receipt + admin-DM gate):
(a) Modify handle_payment_result's test-success branch: instead of creating the final payment inline, transition intent to awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation, post the 'please check your wallet and reply confirmed' prompt in the thread, store the prompt_message_id.
(b) When _handle_pending_recipient_message gets a positive confirmation in awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation: call db_handler.mark_wallet_verified, create the real payment via payment_service.request_payment (is_test=False), transition intent to awaiting_admin_approval, and fire the admin DM.
(c) Auto-skip path: in execute_initiate_payment (and execute_initiate_batch_payment), when a verified wallet exists, create the real payment directly and go straight to awaiting_admin_approval — skip _start_admin_payment_flow (which is test-only). This is a branch on wallet_record.verified_at at intent-creation time, not inside _start_admin_payment_flow.
(d) Admin DM: new helper `_send_admin_approval_dm(payment)` in PaymentUICog that DMs ADMIN_USER_ID with amount/recipient/redacted-wallet/intent_id/jump-link and attaches a new AdminApprovalView keyed by payment_id. On discord.Forbidden → log ERROR, leave intent in awaiting_admin_approval (fail-closed, no public fallback).
(e) New AdminApprovalView class in payment_ui_cog.py, pattern-copied from PaymentConfirmView. Button callback verifies interaction.user.id == ADMIN_USER_ID, calls payment_service.confirm_payment, edits the DM to ✅, follows up with 'payment confirmed, sending', posts 'Payment sent to @user' in the intent's thread, and deletes stale flow messages via safe_delete_messages.
(f) Persistence: PaymentUICog.cog_load gains a _register_pending_admin_approval_views pass that queries admin_payment_intents WHERE status='awaiting_admin_approval' and bot.add_view(AdminApprovalView(...)) for each.
(g) Remove the admin_chat_cog.py:393 send_confirmation_request call. Grants' call at grants_cog.py:719 stays untouched.

PHASE 4 — New agent tools:
(a) Three read-only query tools (query_payment_state, query_wallet_state, list_recent_payments): thin wrappers over existing db_handler helpers with wallet redaction applied via the existing redact_wallet import. Add to TOOLS list in tools.py, dispatch in execute_tool, and list in SYSTEM_PROMPT.
(b) initiate_batch_payment: takes payments=[{recipient_user_id, amount_sol, reason?}, ...]. Per-payment LLM-wallet-injection guard (pop wallet_address/recipient_wallet and warn, same as current execute_initiate_payment). Validate every element first (guild_id, channel_id, amount > 0, no active duplicate for recipient), build the payload list, call db_handler.create_admin_payment_intents_batch. On success, iterate the returned rows and fan out through the same per-payment mechanics as initiate_payment (auto-skip branch for verified wallets OR _start_admin_payment_flow for unverified). Each verified path fires its own admin DM — so a 16-payment batch produces 16 DMs.
(c) upsert_wallet_for_user: takes (user_id, wallet_address, chain='solana', reason?). Admin-only check via a requester context flag. Validate wallet_address via is_valid_solana_address. Call db_handler.upsert_wallet (propagating WalletUpdateBlockedError to the admin reply). Never sets verified_at. On success, DM the admin: 'wallet for @user set to {redacted}, will be verified on next payment'.
(d) SYSTEM_PROMPT addition: a new 'State questions → query first' section instructing the agent to call query_payment_state / query_wallet_state / list_recent_payments before claiming state. Soft guidance, not enforcement. Add the five new tool lines to the 'Doing things' section of the prompt (3 query + 1 batch + 1 upsert).

PHASE 5 — 24h timeout sweep:
New background task (recommend AdminChatCog, since it owns intent lifecycle). @tasks.loop(minutes=15) scans admin_payment_intents WHERE status='awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation' AND updated_at < now()-interval '24 hours', transitions each to manual_review, and DMs admin with full context (intent_id, recipient, tx_signature, wallet).

PHASE 6 — Tests:
Rewrite test_admin_payments.py broadly: new router tests, state-machine tests, keyword parser tests (parametrized), batch atomicity test, upsert admin-check test, auto-skip test, AdminApprovalView persistence + click test, admin DM fail-closed test, gated PaymentConfirmView test, 24h timeout test. Keep pass_to_pass tests (payment state machine, race, reconcile, authorization, solana client, safe delete, invariants, tx signature history) untouched; verify they stay green.

PHASE 7 — Docs:
Append to docs/payments.md and docs/runbook-payments.md: identity routing overview, new states, admin DM approval gate, auto-skip rule, batch tool semantics, upsert tool semantics, and the scoping-reduction note for approved members.

Flagged risks / open questions the plan step should resolve:
 - Batch atomicity when Supabase multi-insert partially fails: confirm PostgREST behavior (should be atomic per-request). If not, fall back to per-row inserts inside a new SQL RPC function wrapped in a txn.
 - The prompt_message_id column already exists on admin_payment_intents but currently tracks only one prompt; the new flow posts TWO prompts (wallet request + 'please check and reply confirmed'). Consider adding a second column (e.g., receipt_prompt_message_id) or reusing prompt_message_id by overwriting — prefer reuse with a comment, since only the latest is needed for safe-delete.
 - The ambiguous-reply counter needs a new integer column (e.g., ambiguous_reply_count default 0) on admin_payment_intents, or store it in a JSONB metadata column if one exists. Verify whether metadata exists; if not, add the integer column in the same migration.
 - AdminApprovalView custom_id collision: use f'payment_admin_approve:{payment_id}' — must not collide with the existing f'payment_confirm:{payment_id}' (they don't, but grep to confirm).
